title: Digitorebutia pectinata
taxonomy: Digitorebutia pectinata is classified within the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae. More specifically, it is a member of the tribe Rebutieae. Within this tribe, it is part of the subtribe Rebutiinae. It is also often referred to as a close relative of the genus Rebutia.

intro: Digitorebutia pectinata is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family (Cactaceae), native to Bolivia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects. The bioactive constituents of Digitorebutia pectinata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Digitorebutia pectinata is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into capsules and tinctures for its purported health benefits.
